what_it_does: Implements a React Build workspace that merges map-extraction and handoff-packaging into one surface, plus a Deploy workspace for staging and publishing, all in a single file.
observations: BuildWorkspace composes <MapsWorkspace embedded/> and <PackageWorkspace embedded/> behind a local view switch.
A journey-style chip row and a segmented button bar both control the same view state ('overview', 'maps', 'package').
BuildOverview renders a two-column summary: mockup thumbnail, extraction stats, readiness progress bar, QA rows, and bundle rows.
ExtractStat is a small local metric card used inside BuildOverview.
DeployWorkspace is defined in the same file and shows pre-flight checks and deployment targets.
The file relies on window globals: MS_DATA, PkgRows, ViContext, MorphStage, Icon, Btn, Panel, QaRow, Chip, ViNote.
Bundle contents and some target details are hard-coded strings rather than derived from data.
The 'building' state is set true by two buttons but never reset or wired to actual export logic.
standouts: Merges two formerly separate pipeline pages into one composed workspace.
Overview reuses shared primitives and the same BundleRow as the Package page.
DeployWorkspace is co-located in build.jsx despite being a distinct workspace surface.
conventions: Reads shared application data from window.MS_DATA.
Uses window-level shared components and hooks (ViContext, MorphStage, PkgRows).
Mixes CSS utility classes with inline style objects.
Calls window.ViContext.set in a mount effect to register workspace context.
concerns: DeployWorkspace is bundled in build.jsx without a clear file boundary.
Bundle counts are hard-coded strings, not computed from pkg.bundles or maps.
The building state is toggled on but never cleared or used for real export flow.
Inline styles repeat magic color values and spacing tokens.
onGoTo('generation') and onGoTo('dashboard') assume external routes exist.
notes: This file is the Build workspace surface: an overview plus embedded maps/package views, with a Deploy workspace tacked on at the end.
questions: What does the embedded prop actually change inside MapsWorkspace and PackageWorkspace?
How is the building state cleared or handled after clicking Build & send?
Where are the missing states Foldback and Project Return generated?
Is DeployWorkspace intended to stay in this file or be split out?
What is the exact contract of window.PkgRows.BundleRow?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to merge map extraction and package assembly into one composed surface; BuildWorkspace does that, but the file also unexpectedly contains the separate DeployWorkspace.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth through composition
intention_signals: The file is trying to collapse a fragmented pipeline into one coherent 'crossing' UI, using composition and shared components so the Build surface stays consistent with Maps and Package while adding an opinionated overview and a deploy hand-off.
novelty: Merges two formerly separate pages into one composed workspace with a journey-style chip navigation and an overview that bridges extraction stats to downstream package readiness.
contribution: Provides a composed Build workspace surface that reuses embedded Maps and Package workspaces, an overview dashboard linking extracted maps to package readiness, and a Deploy workspace for pre-flight checks and target selection.
summary_for_embedding: A React workspace component that composes embedded maps and package views, shows an overview of extracted maps and handoff readiness, and includes a deploy surface for staging and publishing.
intention_for_embedding: To unify map extraction and package handoff into a single Build destination and provide a staging/publish hand-off surface.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/Counterpart-handoff/counterpart/project/src/workspaces/build.jsx::BuildOverview | component | function BuildOverview({ maps, pkg, target, readyCount, totalCount, allReady, onView }) {
symbols.does: Renders a two-column overview of the approved mockup, extracted map stats, package readiness, and bundle contents.
symbols.description: BuildOverview is a local helper component used inside BuildWorkspace's overview view. It displays the target mockup thumbnail, a scene brief, four extraction metric cards via ExtractStat, a readiness progress bar with QaRow items, and a bundle list using window.PkgRows.BundleRow. It receives pre-computed readiness counts and an onView callback to switch to maps or package views.

symbols.code_id: code://counterpart-design/reference/Counterpart-handoff/counterpart/project/src/workspaces/build.jsx::ExtractStat | component | function ExtractStat({ n, label, icon }) {
symbols.does: Renders a small metric card showing an icon, numeric count, and label.
symbols.description: ExtractStat is a local presentational component used by BuildOverview to display counts for layers, semantic nodes, motion beats, and implied assets. It combines an inline-styled icon badge with a display-font number and a muted label.
